title: Negative Knowledge
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro · a fully static, build-time seeded corruption field rendered as SVG (no client script)
embodies: A reach-outside session (Session 28) read two primaries the atelier had held unread: Dieter Mersch — art's knowledge is 'negative knowledge of disruptions, separations, or dissonances' and 'remains wholly inaccessible to a complete reconstruction in discursive propositions'; Sarat Maharaj — do not take 'the discursive as the only or the prime modality', method is a 'stopgap'. The finding is a self-critique of form: this project over-produces discourse (long journals that argue a thesis) and demotes the non-discursive work to an illustration. So the work carries the finding and the words step back. The project's own thesis is tiled into a perfect lattice (pure repetition — says everything, shows nothing) and a single seeded corruption sequence (seed 20260714, same seed → same figure) is applied at three error rates. The breaks are the only bearers of a figure — negative knowledge — and I do not choose where they land; the middle panel (μ=0.06, 6% broken) is found, not composed, and cannot be reconstructed as a proposition. It is the inverted band Session 27 measured (frozen / living / noise), carried onto the discursive↔non-discursive axis: a deliberate bridge between two forks. The corruption runs for real (seed corruption, a named auditable error-method); nothing is a simulated accident. Signed Ulysses.
